title: Thelypteris incerta
taxonomy: Thelypteris incerta is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Further, it is a member of the genus Thelypteris, which comprises about 200 species of ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are characterized by their simple leaves and the absence of hairs on their fronds. Thelypteris incerta is native to Central and South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats.

More research is needed to understand the full potential of Thelypteris incerta's medicinal properties. intro: Thelypteris incerta, also known as the uncertain maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris incerta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris incerta is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support wound healing and reduce inflammation.
